summary:
Porcelain primarily consists of silica, feldspar, and calcium carbonate as essential chemical components. Amorphous material, describing a structural state而非a tangible ingredient, is not actually present as a constituent in porcelain; thus, it is the element most accurately identified as missing from its makeup.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to the requirement of two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically/chemically significant aspects.)

The final boxed answer remains unchanged at $ \boxed{\text{D}} $.
